name: ARCHER2
oneLiner: The UK's national Tier-1 supercomputer, still central for public research while awaiting its successor.
summary: ARCHER2 is the UK's national Tier-1 supercomputing service for academic and government-backed scientific research. It is an HPE Cray EX system delivering 28 PFLOPS peak performance across 5,860 compute nodes with dual AMD EPYC 7742 processors, plus a small GPU development platform with AMD MI210 nodes. Although primarily CPU-focused, its scale makes it valuable for data processing, simulation, and inference workflows that require very large parallel CPU capacity.

keyFacts.hardware: 5,860 HPE Cray EX nodes with dual AMD EPYC 7742 CPUs; small AMD MI210 GPU development partition
keyFacts.funding: National Tier-1 service backed by EPSRC
keyFacts.energy: Modern Cray EX architecture with Slingshot interconnect and shared national operations

access.route: Access is granted through EPSRC's ARCHER2 allocation programme for UK researchers and public-sector partners.
